name: Restaurant Management API
description: Production REST API for restaurant operations — JWT auth, menu and stock, table seating, orders, payments with 16% VAT, and reservations. Deployed with AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis (via REDIS_URL).

problemStatement.problemDescription: Small and mid-size restaurants often track menus, table availability, orders, inventory, and payments in separate tools or spreadsheets. That leads to double-booked tables, stock overselling, inconsistent billing, and no single API for a POS or admin dashboard.
problemList: Tables marked free while an active order is in progress
Menu changes not reflected consistently across ordering channels
Manual VAT and payment totals prone to error
Stock depletion discovered only after orders are placed
Reservation requests handled outside the order/table system

solution.solutionTitle: One API for front-of-house and back-of-house

solutionList.title: JWT-authenticated staff workflows
solutionList.description: Signup, login, logout, and logout-all via SimpleJWT with standardized JSON responses and 8-hour access tokens.

solutionList.title: Table-aware ordering
solutionList.description: Starting an order atomically marks the table unavailable; completing or cancelling releases it.

solutionList.title: Menu with cache-backed categories
solutionList.description: Public dish browsing with Upstash Redis-cached category lists and 15-minute HTTP cache on dish detail.

solutionList.title: Payments aligned to orders
solutionList.description: Payment records auto-created on order completion with 16% VAT via PaymentCalculatorService.

solutionList.title: Reservations with auto table assignment
solutionList.description: Customer reservation requests validate date, party size (max 8), and assign a suitable table when possible.

solutionList.title: Deployed cloud stack
solutionList.description: Dockerized Django API connected to AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis for production cache.

metricsList: 9 Django domain apps (tables, users, menu, orders, payments, stock, reservations, authorization, shared)
Docker image Python 3.12 slim; docker-compose for local dev
DRF throttling 40/minute for anon and authenticated users
Redis cache timeout 600s default; menu categories cached 24h

metrics.label: Django apps
metrics.description: Domain apps plus shared cross-cutting utilities

layers.name: Presentation (clients)
layers.description: POS terminals, admin dashboards, and mobile clients consuming the REST API.
responsibilities: Send JWT Bearer token on protected routes

layers.name: API layer
layers.description: Django 5.1 + DRF monolith exposing /api/v1/ with ViewSets, custom actions, and function-based views.
responsibilities: Business rules in service classes, not views
Unified exception handling and JSON responses

layers.name: Data & cache
layers.description: Persistent storage on AWS RDS PostgreSQL and hot-path cache on Upstash Redis.
components: Upstash Redis — django-redis via REDIS_URL (DB index 1)
responsibilities: Menu category cache and rate-limit counters in Redis

layers.name: Deployment
layers.description: Docker container running Django runserver (dev) or Gunicorn (production placeholder).
responsibilities: Volume mount for live code reload in dev

designPatterns.title: Service layer
designPatterns.description: Domain logic encapsulated in *Service classes (OrderService, PaymentService, DishService, etc.) called from views.

designPatterns.title: Result type
designPatterns.description: Auth flows use Result success/failure objects instead of raising exceptions for expected failures.

designPatterns.title: Repository via ORM
designPatterns.description: Django ORM models with ViewSets for CRUD; custom @action methods for domain transitions (complete, cancel).

designPatterns.title: Unified API response
designPatterns.description: All responses wrapped in success/message/data/timestamp envelope via shared serializers.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Redis for hot paths
scalabilityStrategies.description: Offload menu category cache to Upstash Redis to reduce DB reads on public menu endpoints.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless JWT auth
scalabilityStrategies.description: Horizontal scaling of API containers without server-side session storage.

scalabilityStrategies.title: External managed DB
scalabilityStrategies.description: AWS RDS handles connection pooling and backups independently of the API container.

securityStrategies.title: JWT Bearer authentication
securityStrategies.description: HS256-signed tokens with 8-hour access lifetime; refresh token blacklist on logout.

securityStrategies.title: DRF throttling
securityStrategies.description: Global AnonRateThrottle and UserRateThrottle at 40 requests/minute.

securityStrategies.title: Admin isolation
securityStrategies.description: User and table mutations require Django IsAdminUser (is_staff flag).

securityStrategies.title: Custom JSON exception middleware
securityStrategies.description: JSONExceptionMiddleware ensures unhandled errors return JSON, not HTML stack traces.

cacheStrategies.name: Default Django cache
cacheStrategies.description: django-redis backend pointing to Upstash Redis DB 1 via REDIS_URL

cacheStrategies.name: Test fallback
cacheStrategies.description: LocMemCache when TESTING=True or running manage.py test

architectureFeatures.title: Atomic table locking
architectureFeatures.description: Order creation and completion use database transactions to keep table availability consistent.

architectureFeatures.title: Order-to-payment pipeline
architectureFeatures.description: Completing an order automatically creates a pending payment with calculated VAT.

architectureFeatures.title: OpenAPI-first docs
architectureFeatures.description: drf-spectacular generates live schema consumed by Swagger UI and ReDoc.

requestFlow.title: Client sends request
requestFlow.description: HTTP request to /api/v1/ with optional Authorization Bearer JWT header.

requestFlow.title: Middleware pipeline
requestFlow.description: JSONExceptionMiddleware, RatelimitMiddleware, auth, and DRF throttling process the request.

requestFlow.title: View or ViewSet
requestFlow.description: Permission check, serializer validation, then service layer execution.

requestFlow.title: Data layer
requestFlow.description: PostgreSQL writes for domain state; Redis reads/writes for cache keys.

requestFlow.title: Standardized response
requestFlow.description: ApiResponse JSON envelope returned with success, message, data, and timestamp.

eventFlow.title: Order completed
eventFlow.description: OrderService.complete_order frees table inside atomic transaction.

eventFlow.title: Payment auto-created
eventFlow.description: PaymentService.create_payment_from_order generates line items and 16% VAT totals.

eventFlow.title: Reservation status change
eventFlow.description: Staff PATCH updates reservation status; table assignment validated by ReservationService.

techDecisions.title: AWS RDS + Upstash Redis (external managed services)
techDecisions.problem: Database and cache must survive API container restarts and scale independently.
techDecisions.solution: PostgreSQL via DJANGO_DB_* pointing to AWS RDS; cache via REDIS_URL to Upstash Redis DB 1.
alternatives: In-container PostgreSQL and Redis (docker-compose local only)
techDecisions.outcome: Production deploy uses managed RDS and Upstash; local dev uses docker-compose services.

techDecisions.title: Service layer over fat views
techDecisions.problem: Order/table/payment rules span multiple models and need transactional integrity.
techDecisions.solution: Dedicated *Service classes with atomic blocks called from thin views.
techDecisions.outcome: Testable business logic; clear separation between HTTP and domain rules.

techDecisions.title: django-redis over Memcached
techDecisions.problem: Need a shared cache compatible with cloud Redis and local docker-compose.
techDecisions.solution: Migrated to django-redis with REDIS_URL env var (Upstash in production).
techDecisions.outcome: Single REDIS_URL config works for Upstash, local Redis, and docker-compose.

codeExamples.title: Atomic table reservation on order start
codeExamples.description: OrderService.start_order validates table availability and marks the table taken inside a database transaction — preventing double seating.

files.name: order_service.py
files.explanation: Table availability check and atomic order creation with _take_table.
files.content:
if not table.is_available:
    raise TableNotAvailableForOrder(
        f"Table {table.number} is currently not available."
    )

with transaction.atomic():
    order = Order.objects.create(**validated_data)
    cls._take_table(order)

codeExamples.title: Auto payment creation on order completion
codeExamples.description: When staff completes an order, PaymentService generates line items from order items and calculates 16% VAT totals.

files.name: payment_service.py
files.explanation: Payment.from_order persists the record; ItemService and CalculatorService handle line items and VAT.
files.content:
payment = Payment.from_order(order)
payment.save()
payment_items = ItemService.generate_items_from_order(
    order.order_items.all(), payment
)
ItemService.save_items(payment_items)
CalculatorService.calculate_payment_totals(payment, payment_items)

codeExamples.title: Redis-cached menu categories
codeExamples.description: DishService caches category list in Upstash Redis for 24 hours, reducing DB load on the public categories endpoint.

files.name: services.py
files.explanation: Cache-aside pattern with menu_categories key and 86400s TTL.

codeExamples.title: Redis cache configuration via REDIS_URL
codeExamples.description: settings.py connects django-redis to Upstash (production) or local Redis (dev) through a single REDIS_URL environment variable.

files.name: settings.py
files.explanation: Production uses RedisCache; tests fall back to LocMemCache.

features.title: JWT authentication & session management
features.description: Email-based signup and login with SimpleJWT access/refresh tokens, logout, and logout-all session invalidation.
highlights: Custom JWT claims: user_id, email, role
Standardized ApiResponse wrapper on all auth endpoints

features.title: Table-aware order lifecycle
features.description: Orders atomically reserve tables on start and release them on complete or cancel. Order completion auto-triggers payment creation.
highlights: start_order marks table unavailable inside transaction.atomic()
complete_order validates non-empty order before closing

features.title: Redis-backed menu caching
features.description: Dish categories cached in Upstash Redis for 24 hours. Dish detail responses use 15-minute HTTP cache_page.

codeSnippet.code:
cached = cache.get("menu_categories")
if not cached:
    cached = [...]
    cache.set("menu_categories", cached, timeout=86400)

features.title: Payments with VAT calculation
features.description: Payment records with line items, discounts, and automatic 16% VAT via PaymentCalculatorService. Soft-delete support.

features.title: Reservation workflow
features.description: Public booking requests with validation, staff status updates, and admin CRUD. Max 8 customers per reservation.
highlights: Status flow: PENDING → BOOKED → ATTENDED / NOT_ATTENDED / CANCELLED
Auto table assignment when suitable table available
Staff view for today and tomorrow reservations

features.title: Stock & inventory tracking
features.description: Nested stock items under stock records with IN/OUT transactions that update quantities in real time.

features.title: OpenAPI documentation
features.description: Auto-generated schema via drf-spectacular with Swagger UI and ReDoc. All major endpoints annotated with @extend_schema.

features.title: Docker deployment
features.description: Python 3.12 slim image with docker-compose for local dev (Postgres + Redis). Production connects to AWS RDS and Upstash Redis.
highlights: Deployed state — external RDS + Upstash, not in-container DB

cloudServices.purpose: Production primary database — users, orders, payments, reservations, stock, menu. Connected via DJANGO_DB_NAME, DJANGO_DB_USER, DJANGO_DB_PASSWORD, DJANGO_DB_HOST, DJANGO_DB_PORT.

cloudServices.name: Upstash Redis
cloudServices.purpose: Production cache backend via REDIS_URL (django-redis, DB index 1). Menu categories, rate-limit counters, and general Django cache.

cloudServices.name: Docker host
cloudServices.purpose: Runs restaurant_app container with env-injected secrets. Local dev via docker-compose; production host {{YOUR_DEPLOY_HOST}}.

components.name: Upstash Redis

dockerFiles.description: Production-ready API image — Python 3.12-slim, libpq-dev, installs requirements.txt, exposes 8000.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /restaurant_management_backend
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y libpq-dev gcc
COPY requirements.txt /restaurant_management_backend/
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . /restaurant_management_backend/
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python3", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]

dockerFiles.description: Three-service local stack — db, redis, restaurant_app on app_network.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:latest
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:alpine
    ports: ["6379:6379"]
  restaurant_app:
    build: .
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    depends_on: [db, redis]
    environment:
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/1
      - DJANGO_DB_HOST=db
      - DJANGO_DB_NAME=mydb
      - DJANGO_DB_USER=postgres
      - DJANGO_DB_PASSWORD=postgres
